observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed through the
Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the reconciling items based
on competent documents.

We recommended and the LCE through the (a) Inventory Committee (i) conduct the
semestral physical count of inventories; and (ii) submit the Report on Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on the
NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) maintain Supplies
Ledger Card for each commodity/stock; and (ii) compute the ending inventory and
cost of supplies using the moving average method; and (c) Municipal Treasurer to
(i) maintain Stock Cards for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of
the same; (ii) consolidate monthly the RIS for supplies and materials using the
SSMI; and (iii) submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the
Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE; (b) Municipal Treasurer
to (i) maintain updated PCs; (ii) promptly dispose the unserviceable assets
in accordance with the prescribed procedures; (iii) appropriate funds and
facilitate the registration of vehicles and titling of parcel of land in the name
of the Municipal Government to preclude third party claims over said
properties; (iv) register with the LTO the motor vehicles that have expired
registration; and (v) insure properties with the GSIS; (c) Municipal
Accountant to (i) take – up the necessary accounting entries to derecognize
from the books of accounts the amounts lumped under the unreconciled SL
and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs/SLs tally with the balances of
controlling PPE accounts in the GL; (ii) reverse the journal entries made to
derecognize the PPEs costing ₱4,756,705 without the necessary supporting
documents; (iii) record the appraised value of the donated land; and (iv) set
up receivable accounts for the persons accountable for the missing PPEs; and (d) Municipal BAC to identify gaps that led to the acquisition of vehicles
not registered in the municipality's name and implement necessary changes
to prevent recurrence of the same.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MPDO, Municipal Engineer and
department heads concerned to (i) ensure that all projects funded by national
government agencies are thoroughly planned, with realistic timelines and
implementation strategies; (ii) regularly monitor and evaluate project
implementation to track progress, identify bottlenecks, and ensure timely
completion; (iii) align project implementation schedules with fund release timelines
and enhance coordination among implementing units; and (iv) regularly reconcile
fund transfers, expenditures, and balances with source agencies and internal
records to ensure accuracy and accountability in financial reporting.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal HRM
Officer to (i) continue updating the leave credits of employees of the
Municipality; and (ii) forward the Schedule of Accumulated Leave Balances
to the Municipal Accountant for proper reconciliation and booking; (b)
Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize the provision of Leave Benefits
Payable in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009 dated July 16, 2024;
and (ii) present both current and non-current portions of the Leave Benefits
Payable account in accordance with IPSAS 1 and 39; and (c) Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer take
coordinated actions to increase revenue collections and allocate sufficient
budgetary provisions to facilitate the recognition and payment of the
accounts related to employee benefits.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MTO to (i) utilize the
available contracts and Purchase Orders in assessing the business taxes of the
entities that had transactions with the Municipality; (ii) mandate all business
taxpayers to submit a certified copy of their Income Tax Return (ITR); and (iii)
henceforth, strictly enforce the Revenue Code of Laur; (b) Municipal Treasurer to
examine and verify the books of accounts and pertinent records of business
establishments; and (c) BPLO to verify, assess, and validate the disclosed under-
collection of business taxes from the three samples business establishments.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal HRMO to
(i) formulate and issue approved policy guidelines on the hiring of job order
promulgating the standards for uniform application observing the
provisions of CSC Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002; (ii) ensure that
the hiring of job order is based on specific projects or programs supported by a
detailed plan including the objectives, deliverables and duration; and (iii)
provide approved standard daily wage rate to job order performing the same
function and having the same designation; and (b) Municipal HRMO and
Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012 – 001 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission of required
supporting documents before processing the payment of wages of job order.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) MDRRM Officer, in
collaboration with the MDRRMC, to ensure the maximum utilization of
available LDRRMF on DRRM PPAs, through judicious planning and regular
monitoring of program implementation; and (b) Municipal HRMO to take
appropriate actions to expedite the hiring of LDRRM Assistants to completely
fill -up the mandatory LDRRM positions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the GFPS to (a) continue its
efforts in the mainstreaming of GAD in local operations and governance to
achieve full compliance with GAD laws and regulations; and (b) establish a
GAD database that includes sex-disaggregated data that will be the basis of
gender analysis to identify existing gender issues to ensure that the PPAs to
be formulated were responsive to the said issues.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the rules and regulations set
by the BIR on the withholding and timely remittance of taxes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enforce the immediate settlement
of the remaining disallowances and strictly adhere to the accounting and
auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of
transactions to avoid further suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Property and Accounting Unit to immediately commence the reconciliation of
PPE balances and ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly
recorded in their respective records;

b. Inventory Committee to re-evaluate the noted inconsistencies in the adoption
of the One-Time Cleansing and take note of those procedures that can be re-
applied or make corrective actions; and

c. Inventory Committee to expedite the remaining procedures in the application
of one-time cleansing of the PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to assign additional personnel to prepare complete listing
of taxpayers with the breakdown of the total delinquent taxes as to the
principal amount, interest and penalty to come up with a comparable data with
that of the Municipal Accountant’s record;

b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of
delinquent real property taxes as of year-end against the recorded RPT and
SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the
financial statements;

c. Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the current
year and ensure posting and sending of Notices of Delinquency to all
delinquent taxpayers as required per Section 254(a) of RA No. 7160;

d. Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Government of Batangas
and continue to monitor the status of collections of the delinquent real
property taxes already forwarded to the latter; and

e. Municipal Treasurer to forward a certified list of all real property tax
delinquencies to the Sangunian at year end with indicated reason or reasons
for non-collection or nonpayment and request for assistance to enforce
remedies to collect.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to avoid granting
cash advances near end of year wherein days are limited to fully serve its
purpose and to liquidate. Otherwise, if necessary, instead of cash advance,
direct the Municipal Accountant to accrue and recognize the payable amounts
due to the qualified beneficiaries upon presentation of complete supporting
documents;

b. direct the concerned offices and program proponents and implementers to
establish proper procedure and cut-off on the payouts of assistance, including
early assessment of target beneficiaries and collating necessary supporting
documents, to circumvent any delay in liquidation of cash advances intended for
such; and

c. require the accountable officers to liquidate cash advances within the period
specified in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 supported with the
complete and proper documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
initiate the request for write-off of the dormant Due to NGAs balance of
P106,969.75 following the guidelines and procedures prescribed under the
aforementioned COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry for the overstated amount of
P810.00 in the Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank and the double entry made
in April 12, 2024 amounting to P2,637.36.

b. Municipal Treasurer to submit the duplicate copies of ORs with serial numbers
32180326 to 32180339, 32183109 to 32183141 and 32181206; and

c. Municipal Treasurer to advice all the collectors to strictly practice issuing of
pre-numbered accountable forms in chronological order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere to the provisions of the
IRR of RA No. 9433 by requiring the Human Resources and Management Officer
and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer on identifying and
determining specific positions that are eligible to receive hazard pay with
descriptions as to actual exposure to occupational risks including the duration of
such exposure and assignment to places mentioned in Section 16.1.1 of the IRR,
subject for approval by the Municipal Mayor.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social
Workers mentioned in Annex K to submit proof of actual exposure to occupational
risks and duration of their exposure to substantiate the payments of hazard pay
totalling P503,866.42, equivalent to 20 percent of their basic pay, together with the
documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001, for further review and
evaluation and to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal DRRM Council
to see to it that all available funds for the LDRRM has corresponding program,
project and/or activities incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the rules and regulations set by the BIR on the withholding and timely remittance of taxes.
observations.managementAction: Remit taxes withheld on or before the deadline set

ensure the transfer of the ₱291,981.92 balance from the dormant bank
deposit account to maximize the opportunity to utilize these funds for public
services or projects.

2.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Treasurer to (i) update the RPTAR/TIC and submit the Certified List of
Taxpayers to the Municipal Accounting Office; and (ii) make
representations for the deployment of support staff to assist in the
documentation and collection of tax delinquencies; (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) ensure that the set - up of receivables are based on the
updated RPTAR/TIC.

3.43 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Treasurer to (i) allocate sufficient resources, including personnel and tools,
to conduct thorough and complete physical count; (ii) work towards
maintaining updated PCs; (iii) prepare and submit the corresponding
RPCPPE; (iv) reconcile the result of the count with accounting records; (v)
promptly dispose the unserviceable assets in accordance with the prescribed
procedures; (vi) appropriate funds and facilitate the registration of vehicles
and titling of parcel of land in the name of the Municipal Government to
preclude third party claims over said properties; and (vii) register with the
LTO the motor vehicles that have expired registration; (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) take – up the necessary accounting entries to derecognize
the amounts lumped under the unreconciled SL and ensure that the total
balance of PPELCs/SLs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts
in the GL; (ii) record the appraised value or other appropriate value of the
donated land (iii) provide depreciation for all depreciable assets from the
date of acquisition to the present; and (iv) set up receivable accounts for the
persons accountable for the missing PPEs; and (c) Municipal BAC to assess
the procurement processes to identify gaps that led to the acquisition of
vehicles not registered in the municipality's name and implementing
necessary changes to prevent recurrence.

5.13 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to (i) enhance the financial monitoring and reporting systems to ensure all
funds are accurately accounted for; (ii) promptly refund the unutilized fund
balances of ₱105,934.65 from completed projects to the source agencies; (iii)
revert to the General Fund the unused funds totaling ₱2,205,096.83 that
were allocated to supplement various NGA-funded projects; and (iv)
prepare the request for write-off of the unreconciled old accounts amounting
to -₱23,623.63 in accordance with the procedures prescribed.

6.23 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) and other
concerned officials, to (i) initiate the termination of the two projects and the
recovery of the advance payment made to the contractors; (ii) review the
viability of the unimplemented PPAs; and (iii) ensure the full or optimum
utilization of the 20% Development Fund during the budget year; (b) Bids
and Awards Committee and BAC Secretariat to be prudent in exercising
their functions and ensure compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184
and its RIRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in all
stages of the procurement process; (c) MPDO and Municipal Assessor to
initiate the acquisition of ROW for the purchased land at Brgy. San
Fernando Sur; (d) MPDO to obtain reasonable assurance on the viability of
future projects before funds are committed thereto to preclude various
reprogramming and to ensure overall efficiency in the management of
government funds; and (e) Municipal Accountant to ensure that future
claims against government funds are adequately documented.

7.22 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) OIC – MDRRM
Officer, (i) in collaboration with the MDRRMC, to ensure the maximum
application of available LDRRMF on DRRM PPAs, through judicious
planning, regular monitoring of program implementation, and taking
responsive actions on noted gaps; (ii) endeavor to accurately and timely
report on the status of the fund, in collaboration with the Municipal
Accounting Office; and (c) Municipal HRM Officer (MHRMO) to (i)
facilitate the submission of the proposal for the creation of the Municipal
DRRM Office and its mandatory positions to the sanggunian concerned and
the Civil Service Commission; and (ii) henceforth take appropriate actions
to expedite the hiring of MDRRM personnel in the event that the proposal is
approved.

9.20 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to (a) utilize the contracts and Purchase Orders in assessing business tax on
entities that had transactions in the Municipality; (b) mandate all business
taxpayers to submit a certified copy of their Income Tax Return (ITR), if
any; (c) to examine and verify the books of accounts and pertinent records
of business establishments; (d) prepare a stratified schedule of “presumptive
income level” to approximate the gross receipts of each business
classification; and (e) verify, assess, and validate the disclosed under-
collection of business taxes from two (2) business establishments.

10.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to establish
GAD database which includes sex-disaggregated data to ensure realistic
GAD planning and prioritization of GAD concerns.

11.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the rules and
regulations set by the BIR on the withholding and timely remittance of taxes.

12.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to (a) continuously comply with the requirements
of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 and (b) exert extra efforts to
account the remaining balance of the Due to GSIS account.

13.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to (a) continuously comply with the National Health
Insurance Act by remitting all contributions of the members to the Philippine
Health Insurance Corporation; and (b) exert extra efforts to account the
remaining balance of the Due to PhilHealth account.

14.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the requirements of
R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the
deduction and remittance of the members’ contributions and loan
amortizations to Pag-IBIG; and (b) exert extra efforts to account the
remaining balance of the Due to Pag - IBIG account.

15.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to enforce the immediate settlement
of the remaining disallowances and strictly adhere to the accounting and
auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of
transactions to avoid further suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require a Disposal Committee to dispose
of the unserviceable items.

We also recommended that he require the Municipal General Services Officer to:
a. submit to the Audit Team a plan of when to complete the preliminary activities;
b. update the RPCPPE or prepare the list of existing PPE and compare such with the
updated PPELC of the Accounting Office; and
c. prepare the List of PPE items recorded in the PPELCs but not in the RPCPPE and
List of items included in the RPCPPE but not recorded in the PPELCs necessary
for inventory-taking.

We also recommended that he require the Inventory Committee to finalize the
Physical Inventory Plan and coordinate with the GSO on the progress of the
preliminary activities in order to finally begin with the inventory-taking.

We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of providing
residual value for the depreciable component of the road network systems in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

In view thereof, we recommended and the Management agreed to withhold those
taxes arising from Accounts Payable still outstanding as of date and apply this practice
prospectively.

We recommended and the Management agreed to obligate funds upon perfection of
contracts in order to provide/earmark funds for all commitments or encumbrances
incurred as of every year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned implementing offices
of the immediate utilization of fund transfers received by the Municipality to maximize
the funds and deliver the benefits from the intended
projects/programs/activities.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineering
Office to justify the inclusion of EAO in the approved budget for the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal DRRM Council to
re-assess the disaster risk of the Municipality to formulate more feasible PPAs to be
funded out of the LDRRMF-Preparedness and Mitigation Fund that would lessen the
impact of such risk to maximize its utilization.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. present the duplicate copies of the issued AF No. 58 with serial numbers 4804309
to 4804311; and
b. instruct all accountable officers to remit collections intact daily or not later than
the next working day and to issue ORs strictly in sequential order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10,
1997, whereby no granting of additional cash advance shall be made unless
previous cash advances have been settled;
b. submit within 10 days from receipt hereof to the Audit Team the noted lacking
supporting documents; and
c. liquidate cash advances within the period specified in COA Circular No. 97-002
dated February 10, 1997.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
verify the completeness of the supporting documents before recording liquidation of
the cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal HRMO, in
coordination with concerned Department Heads to:
a. Re-evaluate its existing organizational structure based from organization charts of
each department to easily determine the need number of regular plantilla and
outsourced workers;
b. Formulate uniform policy and procedures in engaging and managing outsourced
workers taking into consideration the relevant issuances of oversight agencies;
and internal controls to ensure that they are performing the required task,
reporting to the official station, and bases of assigning wage rates; and
c. provide a Statement of Duties and Responsibilities to JO workers to clearly
identify their responsibilities and perform their duties efficiently.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the update of daily wage
rate of Job Order workers of the Municipality taking into consideration the daily
minimum wage rate in the private sector, and if funds are available, daily rate of
comparable position in the government sector in accordance with DOLE Wage Order
No. IVA-20.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Budget Officer to
appropriate funds for property insurance of the Municipality and the General Services
Officer to prepare the Property Inventory Form and cause the appraisal of the
properties, and submit them for application of insurance with GSIS to protect the
Management thru indemnification in cases of damage or losses due to fortuitous
events.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. empower the GAD Focal Point System to enhance their knowledge and build their
competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender
mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and
GAD-related tools among others; and
b. require the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance
with the mandate of 4.C.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular
(JMC) No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Administrator to
countersign all the checks drawn by the Municipal Treasurer excluding those checks
charged against the SB budget pursuant to the above cited regulations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. ensure that all collections are
(i) timely remitted by the collectors and covered by the required reports; and
(ii) deposited timely and intact;
b. regularly reconcile cash balances with accounting records;
c. consider closing the dormant bank accounts, and revert the balances in accordance with the provisions of the covering agreement, if any, and pertinent regulations; and
d. see to it that internal control procedures over cash are consistently implemented.

1.15 We also recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant in coordination with the MHO to determine and recognize in the
books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines as at year-end and to
henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same; (b) MHO to
continue spearheading the LGU’s vaccination roll out until the desired local
herd immunity is attained; and (c) BAC Chairman and MHO to prepare and
submit the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 02-2021 in future
procurements of ancillary supplies and services.

2.6 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to continue
adhering to pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the grant of Active
Hazard Duty Pay and Special Risk Allowance to ensure that the payments
are valid and legal.

3.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MHO to ensure
that efficient planning and identification of PPAs is made for future
application of COVID funds; (b) MHO, Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Budget Officer to continue the close monitoring of the utilization
of COVID funds and introduce timely disposition of any noted delays or non-
implementation of PPAs.

4.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct the (i) Municipal
Treasurer to immediately liquidate his cash advances, without prejudice to
the filing of appropriate charges against said official for apparent disregard
of a lawful directive from his superior, as warranted; and (ii) henceforth
endeavor to make timely liquidation his cash advances; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to closely monitor the status of cash advances and take
appropriate action thereon with the end view of a timely recording of
financial transactions.

5.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Treasurer to (i) prepare a verified Certified List of Taxpayers that shall be
the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the setting up of RPT and SET
Receivables for CY 2022; and (ii) prepare the list of delinquencies as at end
of CY 2022 and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the
updating of individual tax payer’s account; and (b) the Municipal
Accountant to (i) coordinate with the offices of MTO and Municipal Assessor
to reconcile the records and establish the actual collectible taxes including
the delinquent accounts; and (ii) record the entry to correct the receivable
accounts. Moreover, we advised that the Municipal Mayor consider the
courses of actions provided in pertinent sections of R.A. No. 7160 in case of
tax delinquencies.

6.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to (a) exert extra efforts in locating documents that are competent to support
the validity of said accounts; and (b) if the documents could no longer be
found, initiate proceedings for the submission of request for write off of said
accounts in the manner prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

7.7 From the foregoing, we recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a)
Municipal Treasurer to: (i) conduct a physical count of all inventory items
regularly at the end of every semester and render a report thereon within
the prescribed deadline; (ii) maintain the required SCs to properly monitor
the movements of inventories; and (iii) reconcile the physical count results
with the Accounting records; (b) Human Resource and Management Officer
(HRMO) to initiate actions to address the inadequacy of staff at the MTO;
and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain the SLCs to closely monitor
the inventories; (ii) reconcile the accounting records with that being
maintained by the MTO; and (iii) effect the necessary adjusting entries, if
any, to correct the year-end balances of inventories reflected in the
financial statements.

8.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Inventory
Committee to complete the physical count of PPEs and prepare the
corresponding RPCPPE; (b) Municipal Treasurer to take appropriate
action on the missing PPE items in accordance with the prescribed
procedures; (c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i)
maintain and reconcile their respective records on property; (ii) prepare
the documents that will be needed to support the request for “one-time”
PPE cleansing as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

9.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a)
Municipal Engineer to (i) initiate the termination of the two projects and the
recovery of the advance payment already paid to the contractors; and (ii)
review the viability of the unimplemented PPAs; (b) BAC members and the

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to the following courses of actions:

strictly comply with Section 53 (e) of the IRR of RA 9184.  Procurement of common-use supplies be first referred to the PS-DBM before resorting thru other mode of procurement.
require the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: No management action taken

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year 2018; and (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant for the determination of uncollected RPT and SET incomes of prior years in accordance with the prescribed procedures; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) set up RPT and SET Receivables for each year corresponding to the lists and make necessary adjustments as warranted; and (ii) henceforth observe the prescribed accounting procedures in recording RPT and SET receivables and incomes.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Heads of Offices of Accounting and Treasury to (i) complete the conduct of physical inventory of PPEs within a set timeline during the ensuing year; (ii) regularly report to the Office of the Mayor the status of the said physical validation and to suggest appropriate action for the concerns encountered in the process;  (iii) render  the corresponding RPCPPE; and (iv) settle the disparities between the RPCPPE and the accounting records; (b) assign additional staff to handle the maintenance of property cards at the Treasury Office; (c) require the Disposal Committee to (i) submit to his Office the proposed guidelines and procedures in the disposal of unserviceable properties consistent with those set forth in pertinent COA regulations; and (ii) facilitate disposal of said assets in accordance with the approved guidelines and procedures.

We recommended and the Management concurred that the Municipal Treasurers Office (MTO) strictly observe the rules and regulations on the receipt and deposit of collections. The Municipal Treasurer should exercise close supervision over the collection activities to prevent the incurrence of similar findings in the future, thereby, avoiding the possible ill effect of such malpractice.

We recommended and the Management concurred that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) locate the reported missing vehicle and, if not found, determine accountability therefor; and (b) register all LGU-owned motor vehicles with the LTO and insure the same with the GSIS as required by law and existing regulation.

We recommended and Management concurred that the Local Chief Executive instruct the: (a) Municipal Planning Development Officer and Municipal Engineer (i)  to conduct project planning with reasonable approximation and matching of possible risks with appropriate responsive actions; (ii) include PPAs in the Local Development Project that are technically viable; and (iii) regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions can be introduced at the earliest time possible; and (b) the members of the BAC to align the timelines of procurement processes with the targets indicated in the Annual Investment Plan to ensure timely implementation and completion development projects.
Likewise, management is advised to coordinate with the DILG for possible conduct of training on the revision of CLUP.

We recommend and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive to: (a) exert efforts to collect from concessionaires and send demand letters to concessionaires especially those inactive concessionaires and those with large amount of indebtedness; (b) likewise, start collecting guaranty deposits from concessionaires upon connection which could be used to offset against any unpaid balances for active accounts of more than 60 days or any unpaid balance from inactive accounts; (c) further, strictly observe the disconnection policy of the LWS and avoid granting additional grace period or by collecting through installments; and (d) consider promulgating Operational Manual/Policy for Local Water System or update the Agreement for Water Services between the LWS and the concessionaires

We recommended and Management concurred that the Local Chief Executive: (a) issue policy guidelines in the hiring of JO workers in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292, COA rules & regulations, and existing labor laws; (b) determine the ideal and realistic personnel complement of the Municipality following the mandate of the provisions in Sections 76 & 77 of RA 7160; otherwise, follow the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern instead of hiring unnecessary and excessive number of JO workers; and (c) further, if merited under existing Civil Service rules and regulations, evaluate the performance of said JO workers for possible appointment to permanent regular positions.

We recommended and Management concurred that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Municipal Budget Officer to strictly monitor charges to approved budgets for fuel and lubricants, or for any budget item, for that matter, to prevent incurrence of budget overdrafts; (b) concerned heads of offices to justify the seeming excessive/unreasonable fuel consumption of the identified vehicles, which we will evaluate; and (c) Municipal Engineer to establish and implement effective control/processes on the use of government vehicle and in the withdrawal of fuel for their consumption.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to procure: (a) common-use supplies from PS-DBM before resorting to other modes of procurement; and (b) prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan the passing of ordinance/s that will define the SWM policies that are LGU-specific and shall include, among others, the (i) provision of incentives; and (iii) the declaration of certain acts as prohibited with their corresponding penalties; (b) issue corresponding orders that will operationalize these policies that will cover, among others, the identification of (i) the offices concerned; (ii) their related mandates, functions and responsibilities; and (iii) the operating procedures; (c) prioritize the acquisition of land to be used for MRF and as temporary holding area of Residual Containment Bins; and (d) impose timelines for the implementation of SWM programs, monitor the same and address the noted lapses.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) send the key officials of LCPC to further training on R.A. No. 9344; (b) instruct the LCPC in coordination with the concerned LGU offices to (i) prepare a comprehensive plan on child delinquency prevention and attribute budget therefor in amount equivalent to 1% of IRA; and (ii) oversee its proper implementation as provided for under Section 15 of the said law.

We recommended and Management concurred that the Local Chief Executive direct the: (a) official concerned in charge of the preparation of LDRRM accomplishment report to indicate therein the corresponding targeted quantitative and qualitative program, project and activity, date of actual implementation, and the actual costs incurred to facilitate determination of efficiency in program implementation and effectiveness of the projects accomplished; (b) Human Resources and Management Officer to (i) prioritize the placement of a fulltime MDDRM Officer who will focus on core functions of the MDRRMO and the hiring of the three mandatory staff personnel to handle the administration, training, research and planning, and operations and warning, to provide adequate support to DRRM activities; and (ii) provide necessary interventions to equip personnel of the required competencies necessary to man and run the ERT and, henceforth, constitute the ERT; (c) Municipal Planning and Development Officer to fast track and monitor the preparation of the Municipality’s Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP), Comprehensive Land-Use Plan (CLUP), and to ensure that disaster risk management activities are integrated therein; and (d) Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for LDRRMF to facilitate monitoring of fund utilization.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the GAD Focal Person: (a) to continue to comply with the allocation of the prescribed budget for GAD and the preparation of a relevant GAD Budget and Plan pursuant to the pertinent laws and regulations on GAD; and (b) monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with GAD Plans and Budget as reviewed by the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG).
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended and the Management concurred to continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended and the management agreed to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended the Management to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.
We recommended and Management concurred that the Local Chief Executive direct the persons liable to settle/refund immediately the amount of unsettledisallowances as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
terminate the contracts of the consultants whose services are only duplication of the functions of regular employees or whose works could be performed by existing plantilla personnel;

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant will be more vigilant in the verification of the supporting documents on services rendered by the job orders.
